candidates.direction_name: Port Grouped-Row Traversal Into The Non-PTX 128x128 Sibling
ranking_notes: The active PTX-local families have now consumed two consecutive rounds for one sub-threshold gain and one slight regression, which is enough evidence to demote them below a real family pivot.
This is the cleanest off-branch move that still preserves grouped-row locality and the 128x128 hot-band geometry instead of reopening larger geometry pivots.
It is ranked ahead of the sibling export-scratch trim because the sibling path itself should be validated before spending another round trimming its export surface.
candidates.hypothesis: Rounds 7 and 8 on the restored grouped-rows-4 PTX surface produced the clearest saturation signal yet for the active PTX-local micro families. The export-address cleanup established a new best custom at 24.164352 ms, but only by 0.016304 ms and still as PASS_FLAT; the follow-up control-path issue-grouping tweak then regressed slightly to 24.170400 ms, again as PASS_FLAT. Across both runs the signature barely moved: active warps stayed pinned at 16.61-16.62, barrier stayed in the 5.42-5.45 band, and long-scoreboard stayed in the 7.24-7.26 band. That is enough evidence to stop spending the next round on another tiny PTX-local retime. The best next family is to preserve grouped-row locality but pivot off the active PTX microkernel onto the non-PTX 128x128 sibling, which historically stayed reasonably close to the best PTX surface while materially changing the hot-band control/export path.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: The active PTX control/export surface looks saturated; the next opportunity is preserving grouped-row locality while changing the hot-band control/export implementation family away from the PTX microkernel.
candidates.risk: Medium. This changes the dominant hot-band family rather than another micro-tweak, but it still stays inside the existing grouped-row 128x128 geometry instead of reopening the more disruptive 256x128 or full-band families.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the 24.164352 ms best-known PTX run
correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the non-PTX sibling still trails the active PTX best by a clearly material margin without improving correctness or the stall mix enough to justify staying off the active PTX branch.

candidates.direction_name: Trim The Grouped-Row 128x128 Sibling Export Scratch To The PTX-Style Single Stage
ranking_notes: This is the strongest historically positive follow-on once the sibling 128x128 path is active.
It stays below dir_01 because trimming the sibling export scratch is only coherent if the sibling branch itself is selected or restored first.
It is ranked ahead of another PTX-local fallback because rounds 7 and 8 already showed the active PTX-local micro-families flattening out on the restored grouped-rows-4 surface.
candidates.hypothesis: If the sibling 128x128 control family is the right escape hatch, the cleanest bounded follow-up inside that branch is already known: historical run 1181247 improved its sibling base from 24.449024 ms to 24.422464 ms by replacing the generic two-stage sibling c_shared export path with the PTX-style single-stage per-warp scratch lifetime. That makes this the best second-ranked family for round 9: it keeps the same grouped-row sibling idea as dir_01 but attacks the most concrete residual cost on that branch instead of immediately reopening broader PTX-local retimes again.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Shared export/writeback overhead on the grouped-row non-PTX 128x128 sibling, especially its heavier generic two-stage c_shared export path.
candidates.risk: Medium. This is bounded and historically evidence-backed, but on the current source it likely requires routing the grouped-row sibling into the active hot-band path first or alongside the scratch trim.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the 24.164352 ms best-known PTX run
shared-memory footprint / allocation in the sibling path
correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the sibling export-scratch trim cannot improve on its own historical 24.422464 ms band or if the extra routing/glue required to activate it overwhelms the bounded export-side gain.

ranking_notes: This keeps one active-branch fallback alive in the diagnosis without pretending the family should stay ranked ahead of a real off-branch pivot.
The current source already contains the strongest historically positive one-sync handoff cleanup, so another retime is now a much lower-confidence move.
It stays in third place because the frontier still carries it and it remains more coherent than reopening export cleanup or the already-flat PTX control subfamily yet again.
candidates.hypothesis: The barrier/handoff family remains in the set as the lowest-ranked active-PTX fallback, but only after the loop just spent two rounds proving that the current PTX-local micro-families are close to saturated on the grouped-rows-4 surface. A future retime here would have to be materially narrower and better targeted than the already-baked one-sync handoff plus export cleanup path in the current source. That makes it a hold-in-queue direction rather than the next recommended move: if the off-branch sibling pivot is rejected or fails cleanly, this family still offers one last on-surface scheduler lever without reopening the already weak control-order variants.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Residual wait-group / barrier cadence in the active PTX hot-band loop, after the currently baked handoff and export cleanups have already been absorbed.
candidates.risk: Moderate. The family is bounded, but the current source already includes the strongest obvious handoff cleanup, so another retime can easily reshuffle stalls without beating the current best-known PTX run.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the 24.164352 ms best-known PTX run
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if another bounded handoff retime only trades barrier against scoreboard again without changing runtime meaningfully.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
